The art style is interesting though I think the map looks so lifeless. The buildings look drab and everything has this washed out look, it needs some life and colour. I'm guessing like Dota they're going to put in those little details like maybe average citizens walking around in the side sections. I remember going past the a newsstand outside the base, and it seemed genuinely interesting with the voicelines talking about news in the world of the game. But blink and you'd miss it with all the drab surrounds. My main issue with how the game looks from an art perspective, and basic UI perspective is the buy menu, which is genuinely just text boxes at this point. Not only does the buy menu look confusing and not intuitive, it also looks like a trashy text box UI made out of Word. The game is just too early to be in what's pretty much an unannounced beta. Take it away for a year, figure out what to do gameplay wise (cause currently the game design is still in flux) and be a little further along on the polish, and then rerelease. I can see why people try it for a month or so then bail. Deadlock is nowhere near close to even beta yet.

if you don't think leveling up and skill tree make an RPG then that's fair.... but what does? All games involve you playing a role. Most games these days have a story. Hell you even play the character and chose the dialog and have side quests. It's not the leveling or skill tree, we established that... What does that leave? where to you draw the line? Seems to me that you decide a game is an RPG based on one of two things... Multiple Classes and Turn Based combat.

I would like to recommend you Chaos;Head NoaH... but the switch version really sucks. The only good way to play it is on PC, even though that version sucks as well (they're the same one) So why am I recommending you this game then? Because there's a fix. The problem is that the translation from japanese to english sucks, tons of stuff was censored or straight up cut out of the game and it's really buggy. But the Committee of Zero (who do stuff like fan patches for the Science-Adventure games which Chaos;Head and Steins;Gate are a part of) made a really good patch, which brought back the censored and cut content, fixed the bugs, gave the game a proper translation etc. This game REALLY shouldn't be played on switch (unless you want to mod your switch and download the patch on it), but if someone asks for a psychological horror VN I can't not recommend Chaos;Head because it is such a good game. If you (or someone else reading this) might be interested, there's a pinned post on r/steinsgate which you can read, providing explanation on how to download the patch, as well as explaining what the Science-Adventure Series is. And if someone read all of this, thank you so much :)

I have a pretty basic "gaming" laptop, so I'm limited to old games or low spec games. That being said, I'm wanting to try out indie turn based games if ya'll happen to have any recommendations! Ideally big on story, party based, combat system that is simple to get into but open to experimentation if wanted, no grid based tactical games (unsure if those are the right words to describe that) like Disgaea, and no game where you run around the battlefield and have to tap buttons for combos like the Tales series (though I adored Tales of the Abyss story and characters even if I hated the gameplay) Similar games I've played in the past that I've enjoyed: Final Fantasy IV and V, Chrono Trigger (don't have the steam version), Lunar Silver Star Story Complete and Lunar 2 Eternal Blue Complete (planning on purchasing the remasters coming up), Parasite Eve, Xenogears, and Thousand Arms. Games I'm considering: Super Lesbian Animal RPG, Omori, and Octopath Traveler I and II

>That's impossible given that supply lines from America to the East China Sea would be insufficient for supply given how China can maintain recovery and operations far quicker with that logistical advantage. Their forces would be destroyed, there would be nothing for them to send. The Us supply lines would be fine, as there are both plenty of US bases in the region that are well stocked with supplies, but also plenty of US allies in the region that can help with logistics. Meanwhile, China would be effectively cut off from the rest of the world until hostilities ended and recompense was made. Again, this is not a situation that would be in US interests, it would go poorly for the US overall, it would just go much *worse* for China. Whatever negative consequences the US faced, China would be harmed double.

Help! PS5 Disc Drive Leaving Massive Scratches On My Discs Hi, My PS5 disc drive has started to cause massive scratches to my discs. This started a month ago and has gotten so bad i’ve had to buy a separate blu-Ray player after one Blu-ray was scratched so badly it stopped playing entirely. I’ve had a look around on Reddit and google and it seems I’m not the only one whose disc drive has started to do this. The only fixes posted were to lay the console horizontal, but mine is already laid flat. There haven’t been any changes to the room arrangement nor has the PS5 been damaged in anyway (visible on the outside at least) that could have caused this. For reference this is just a regular PS5 with disc drive, not a PS5 pro with the detached disc drive. Hopefully someone out there has a fix?

As a pc player I already lost all hype when I saw there is no release date for PC. I know it’s GTA 6 but no game is worth staying hyped up for 6 months - an year hoping for it to release on your platform. On top of it I don’t think the single player aspect could have any revolutionary changes or anything anyway, gta v was revolutionary for the time it released but the only thing keeping it relevant for last 10 or so years was the online aspect. So the only thing I’m still a bit interested in is the online aspect but based on the decisions they’ve taken for the current GTA online with the subscription service and other anti consumer changes while also potentially making online mode exclusive as well for months/years, it’s not looking good for it. It’s probably still gonna make billions from the general public but personally I’m probably skipping out on it for a few years anyway I guess. Maybe after a few years it’s gonna go on a sale or something and I’ll try it out with the squad.

You can already use a mouse as the switch also supports USB mice. Now you can develop a game with the assumption that every Switch 2 that ships also comes with an option for mouse control out of the box. What these "geniuses" (read; idiots) in these corporations don't get is that raising their prices puts them out of business. They're not struggling because games are becoming more expensive to make; they're struggling because games are more expensive to make, ***and*** they've made a habit of alienating their customers. They are taking a problem of game development, and compounding it with cutting off their income source. You've got a general audience that already can't afford games on day 1 a lot of the time, and most gamers are sick of these companies crap on top of that. Many gamers spend day 1 prices for games probably once a year. I'm personally far less than that, primarily because the so-called "AAA" games industry is incapable of releasing a stable product at this point, and it's a waste of money. *It has never been easier to pass up a game* than it is right now. GTA6 is not going to be any different. In fact, I fully expect it to be a circus on day 1. If these corpos want to rebound in the games industry, they need to: 1. Stop releasing complete garbage. 2. Stop nickel-and-diming players with microtransactions. 3. Stop pushing BS that gamers don't want to deal with. 4. Stop calling gamers names when their garbage product fails. 5. Stop pretending gamers are the problem with the games industry. 6. Hire people interested in making good games, instead of people only interested in making political statements, who hate gamers, and/or make the work environment toxic to work in. 7. Kiss gamers' feet for the next ten years to make up for all the BS from the previous ten years. Which would lead to... 8. EARN BACK CUSTOMER TRUST. It's not rocket science. It's not even chemistry. This is "The Difference Between Living and Non-Living Things 101 (for dummies)". And they're failing the class.

1-The PS4. I've been playing this one for 10 years. I love the look of the machine. I love the innovations in the controller: the share button is a mini-revolution for the console gamers, being able to save videos and screenshots, and stream at the press of a button; the touchpad is sadly underused but a great addition. And it's with this console that I got to realize my dream: playing in VR! Of all the console that I've had since my GameBoy, it's my fav...but that might change over time because of my second fav... 2-The Steam Deck. I bought it when Valve put the LCD version on sale last year...whoaw this is such a good investment! It's pretty much the portability of the Switch (with caveats) with the power of roughly a PS4 Pro, and the freedom to play pretty much anything like a PC. What it means is, right now, this console can play literally ALL THE GAMES FROM MY PERSONAL TOP 30. Its limits are "just" the battery that can be depleted in about 3hours on more demanding games; the docking/big picture mode not as plug and play as it could be, still work though; and yeah, the newer AAA titles with RTX are going to struggle. But damn, what a great machine! 3-The PS2. The PS1 got me into gaming for real. I loved my GameBoy, loved my MegaDrive 2. But the 3D on PS1 showed me "wow, you can BE in these games". And the PS2 was a continuation with improvements in all places. There are a lot of neat features on it (like the DVD drive) but what makes is so legendary is just the library of amazing games...there is probably at least 100-150 games I could pick without a single dud. Oh and the console looks amazing also, whatever version we're talking about. 4-Xbox360. Baring the fact that I bought it on launch and had 4 RROD in the first two years of this console (yes, I got five different 360 between 2005 and 2007, the last one is still alive), I loved this thing. The design is cool, but not especially great. But the controller is probably the best I ever used with the Dualshock 4; and the features...the online was brilliantly implemented, easy to use, easy to create chat, invite friends etc; the marketplace, that also helped with XNA, to popularize and even create the indie game market for console players (when it was limited to "obscure" websites before); the achievements system! And the library of games was great: a lot of it was also on PS3, let's say that, but a lot of it worked better on 360. 5-The Switch. I liked the WiiU very much, but it had so major flaws in the design. The Switch just knocked it out of the park. It's light, it's soooo ingenious, in concept and design, and every single thing it does works so well. Docking/undocking is done in a second, using any joycon configuration simply works (we got to play a 4 player CTR cup with 4 joycons at New Year's eve it was very fun), and the library of games is full of great titles...the only reason it's not my favorite is because anything 3D not made by Nintendo (or made by Nintendo also at times) is struggling to keep a decent framerate. The console is great, but it lacks just a tiny bit of raw power for me to play my fat-ass open world games. Yes, I know a "cloud" version of some AAA games exist, but it's not ideal for me. The exclusives are really great though. Honorable mention: The DS Lite (black or white version) probably my favorite design in tech ever with the classic white iPod.

4k is a niche because Windows scaling is, well, crap. So you may have a 4k screen, but UI elements (even if you enable display scaling mode for UI elements) will look too small/too weird in many places. So there's little point in getting a 4k monitor at a reasonable size (27-30") when you wouldn't be able to use it for anything other than gaming. Combined with extremely high cost of decent IPS 4k monitors with high refresh rates AND with the hardware needed to pull of decent performance.. and it's simply not worth it. You're looking at a 4090 just to get good frame rates at 4k/120Hz. 1080p is still the standard on low/medium hardware, and 2k (1440p) is the sweet spot on good hardware. Case in point: I have a 3080 Ti and I'm only getting ~70 fps in Starfield in busy outdoor areas at 1440p.

> when im in game the glyphs are shows as xbox glyphs That's because Steam Input works by emulating an XInput device. You'll only get Playstation glyphs if: * a game has native Dualshock/Dualsense support and you disable Steam Input. * the developers have properly implemented newer Steam Input settings that detect your controller and override the displayed glyphs. * there's an in-game setting to choose the glyphs you want. * you find a mod to replace the glyphs.

Yeah, I thought about that today as well! Imagine playing shooters and being basically able to use the right JoyCon as a mouse. Since you basically just turn it 90 degrees and use the shoulder buttons as the two main mouse buttons, your thumb can still stay on the face buttons, so you wouldn’t even "loose“ access to them! Maybe even hold the whole rest of the Switch2, or just the other JoyCon in the holder, to have a hybrid controller and mouse playstyle. Think about potentially playing Prime 4 like that! And of course all those (3)DS games that need a touchscreen, you could simply use the right JC as a mouse. If the screen of the Switch2 can stream to the TV or dock, it might even be possible to use it kinda like a WiiU tablet… There is certainly a lot more potential behind the new mouse functionality, than just using it to browse the eShop! Time will tell, if Nintendo and 3rd parties will use it, of course. EDIT: Just took my JoyCons and make-believe used the right one turned 90 degrees and on a flat surface. It really works! (From a physical point of view, if you want.) The right stick could be mouse wheel, the face buttons are still easily accessible with your thumb and the shoulder buttons are under your index.

Tbh, people aren’t that great at actually setting up games and knowing what they are looking at. Cyberpunk by default has mouse acceleration and smoothing on. That alone makes the game feel laggy however turning it off is like night and day regarding responsiveness. So, does Frame Gen make noticeable input lag? Yes and no. It really depends on what the base frames are. If you are at 60fps in Cyberpunk and you enable FG, it will actually make 45 real frames and FG up to 90ish. It feels extremely shitty because you have the input latency of 45fps but perceiving 90fps. I mean, it’s more sluggish than RDR2’s animations. But, if you have 120fps and then enable FG you’ll barely feel the difference. Input lag and FG is better viewed from a “what percentage of latency did it increase” rather than “how many ms did latency increase.” In other words, you’re unlikely to feel a 5% difference but you will definitely feel a 20% difference.

They are somewhat comparable in difficulty but Jokerless was definitely more difficult in my experience. The amount that Jokers can add to your score and your economy is massive and far outweighs the -1 hand on Black Deck. Even on Gold Stake needing 4x the score to win, this is mitigated completely by having one or two xMult Jokers. To put it this way: in both Jokerless and Black Deck Gold Stake, it can be very difficult just to survive past Ante 3. In Black Deck Gold Stake, if you do survive to Ante 3, you probably have a good setup and a pretty good chance of winning the run. In Jokerless, the scaling doesn't let up at all and every Ante will be a struggle. (aside from the very specific lucky case where you managed to duplicate a Glass/Polychrome/Red Seal card a dozen times)

Maybe there are diminishing returns. It has 30% more CUDA cores so you would expect at least 30% improvement over the 4090 in pure rasterisation without any DLSS, just native. However, rasterisation is not the only thing. People keep buying nvidia for DLSS and ray tracing. It seems there the 5090 would be an even bigger upgrade over the 5090. 5090 is the best gpu money can buy. Should a 4090 owner upgrade? Probably not. Unless they want the very best performance and that would be the 5090.

I appreciate how Hideo Kojima will post updates on Death Stranding 2 on Twitter. If he's spending a day in the recording studio with the voice actors he will say so. Or if he's just testing the game he'll say that. Usually just very tiny mentions of what he himself is doing with regards to the game Which is honestly fine, it's something- and it's not silence, which is what I'm grateful for Of course he won't update people on day-to-day progress of the game. No one is expecting him to anyways. But his tweets are constant enough that I know it's progressing at a decent rate and there's no reason for me to be impatient about not receiving a release date or a new trailer. When a developer is completely silent is when I get frustrated. I can't imagine how annoying it must be to be a Hollow Knight fan, I've noticed they've waited for quite a long time and haven't heard much at all. Not that I think their childish outbursts are reasonable, just saying they could be prevented by small updates by the marketing guy every several months at least. Doesn't seem like a big ask to me.

D10 players, even the "Casual" ones (260+ hrs), are often playing in a team of 4 regular friends, faction specialists or stealth players.
